262.reason: [This line incorrectly appends a second '&' to form "&&", but the input only had a single '&'. The token should not be emitted as AND when it's actually an invalid single '&'. Emitting a fake AND token corrupts output and violates spec.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This line incorrectly appends a second '&' to content, resulting in "&&" even though only a single '&' was read; it should append the actual character read (which is not '&'), or better yet, not generate a token at all for erroneous single '&']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This line incorrectly appends a second '&' to form "&&", but the input only had a single '&'. The token should not be emitted as AND when it's actually an invalid single '&'. Emitting a fake AND token corrupts output and violates spec.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This line incorrectly appends a second '&' to content, making it "&&", which is misleading because the input only had a single '&'. The token should reflect the actual lexeme read, which is just "&".]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [This incorrectly appends a second '&' to form "&&", but the input only had a single '&'. The token should not be emitted as AND when it's actually an invalid single '&'. Emitting a fake AND token is wrong; instead, no valid token should be generated for erroneous single '&' or '|'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This line incorrectly appends a second '&' to content, but the actual character read was not '&'; it should append the original single '&', which is already in content. The current logic duplicates '&', producing "&&" as token content for a single '&', which is wrong.]

291.reason: [Incorrectly emits an OR token for a single '|'; should not produce a valid token when encountering lexical error 'a']

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrectly emits an OR token for a single '|'; should not produce a valid token when encountering lexical error 'a']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrectly emits an OR token for a single '|'; also, the content string is wrong.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: emitting an OR token for a single '|', which is invalid. This misrepresents the input and violates the specification that single '&' or '|' should trigger error 'a' and not produce a valid AND/OR token.]

277.reason: [Same error as above: incorrectly constructs "||" from a single '|', then emits an OR token. For error type 'a', only the error should be recorded; no invalid token should be added to the token stream.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same error as above: incorrectly constructs "||" from a single '|', leading to wrong token content]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same error as above: incorrectly constructs "||" from a single '|', then emits an OR token. For error type 'a', only the error should be recorded; no invalid token should be added to the token stream.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same error as above: appends a second '|' to make "||" when only one '|' was in the input, misrepresenting the actual lexeme.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: appending an extra '|' to make "||" when only one '|' exists in input. This creates an incorrect OR token for an invalid single '|'. The lexer should not produce a valid token for this case.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue as with '&': this incorrectly appends a second '|' to content, resulting in "||" even though only one '|' was present. The content should remain as a single '|'.]
